prompt: Which courses have the most prerequisites?

inputData.question: Which courses have the most prerequisites?

response.bodyRaw:
{"status":"success","steps":{"input":{"question":"Which courses have the most prerequisites?"},"sql":{"payload":{"question":"Which courses have the most prerequisites?"},"startedAt":1771855532620,"status":"success","output":{"reasoning":"The question asks for courses with the most prerequisites. I need to join the courses table with the prerequisites table, count the number of prerequisites per course, and order by that count in descending order. The prerequisites table has a course_id column that references the course being taken, and a prerequisite_course_id that references the prerequisite course. I'll group by course and count the prerequisites, then order by count descending to find which courses have the most prerequisites.","sql":"
SELECT 
  c.id,
  c.code,
  c.name,
  COUNT(p.id) AS prerequisite_count
FROM courses c
LEFT JOIN prerequisites p ON c.id = p.course_id
GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
ORDER BY prerequisite_count DESC, c.code ASC
","assumptions":["The prerequisites table stores prerequisites where course_id is the course that has prerequisites, and prerequisite_course_id is the course that must be taken first","Courses with no prerequisites will have a count of 0","Results are ordered by prerequisite count descending, then by course code for deterministic ordering"]},"endedAt":1771855562583},"run-raw-sql":{"payload":{"sql":"
SELECT 
  c.id,
  c.code,
  c.name,
  COUNT(p.id) AS prerequisite_count
FROM courses c
LEFT JOIN prerequisites p ON c.id = p.course_id
GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
ORDER BY prerequisite_count DESC, c.code ASC
"},"startedAt":1771855562588,"status":"success","output":{"ok":true,"sql":"SELECT

GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
ORDER BY prerequisite_count DESC, c.code resultRaw.sql:
SELECT 
  c.id,
  c.code,
  c.name,
  COUNT(p.id) AS prerequisite_count
FROM courses c
LEFT JOIN prerequisites p ON c.id = p.course_id
GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
ORDER BY prerequisite_count DESC, c.code ASC

result.sql:
SELECT 
  c.id,
  c.code,
  c.name,
  COUNT(p.id) AS prerequisite_count
FROM courses c
LEFT JOIN prerequisites p ON c.id = p.course_id
GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
ORDER BY prerequisite_count DESC, c.code ASC
